bullet_points: Partition the 1523 patches into fixed train (80%), validation (10%), and test (10%) splits using a seeded random number generator and save the indices to disk.
Compute per-channel mean and standard deviation statistics from the training split only and save these to disk for consistent use in all subsequent steps.
Implement a data loader class that handles loading, normalization, and augmentation (rotations/flips) for the multi-frequency input maps and ground truth.
Pre-calculate and cache unit conversion factors (e.g., jysr2uk) to minimize redundant computation.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Implement a robust data loading pipeline with fixed data splits and normalization statistics.

bullet_points: Implement the U-Net architecture using PyTorch, incorporating gated cross-attention layers to fuse SO-LAT and Planck channels.
Define the composite loss function L = L_L1 + lambda_1 * L_spec + lambda_2 * L_corr, with L_spec using utils.powers for l in [1000, 5000].
Train the model on the training split, monitoring validation loss at each epoch with early stopping (patience of 10 epochs).
Save the final model checkpoint with the best validation loss to disk.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Construct and train the multi-scale U-Net with gated cross-attention.

bullet_points: Perform beam deconvolution on all input frequency maps to a common resolution before forming the linear combination.
Configure the cILC to null CIB spectral signatures while preserving the tSZ signal.
Apply the cILC to the test set to generate baseline tSZ maps.
Compute MSE and bias metrics for the cILC baseline to facilitate comparison with the SR-DAE.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Implement the constrained Internal Linear Combination (cILC) as a linear benchmark.

bullet_points: Load the best SR-DAE checkpoint and perform inference on the test set, ensuring outputs are converted back from normalized space to native dimensionless units.
Compute residual maps R = tSZ_true - tSZ_pred and cross-correlation coefficients between R and input CIB maps for both models.
Compute the transfer function T(l) for both models to quantify resolution gain.
Compute the tSZ power spectrum for both models and compare against the FLAMINGO ground-truth power spectrum across angular scales.
Generate and save plots: transfer functions T(l), residual maps, CIB cross-correlation, and power spectrum comparisons.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Evaluate the SR-DAE and cILC models and generate all required figures.

bullet_points: Synthesize quantitative results (MSE, bias, transfer function values at specific l bins, cross-correlation coefficients) from the SR-DAE and cILC comparisons.
Report all key metrics as specific numerical values with patch-to-patch standard deviations or confidence intervals.
Interpret the transfer function analysis and the power spectrum comparison.
Discuss the effectiveness of the SR-DAE in disentangling tSZ from CIB and noise, and provide a final assessment of the model's performance and physical validity.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Write the final Results section of the research report.
